title: Kanban: Force-Done button, stuck-task indicator, and manual reconcile trigger
description:
Three related dashboard improvements to make stuck-task recovery and system health visible without needing CLI intervention:

1. **Force-Done button**: On KanbanCard, add a context action (or in TaskModal) to force a task to Done. This writes `status: done` to the canonical task file via a new API endpoint `POST /api/control/tasks/force-done` which takes `{task_id, project_id}`, sets canonical status=done, clears claimed_by, and deletes the runtime state file. Useful when a task is visually stuck.

2. **Stuck-task indicator**: In KanbanCard, when `pipeline_state === 'running'` and `last_heartbeat` is older than 30 minutes, show a warning badge (e.g. amber pulse dot) to visually surface tasks that may be stuck. The boards API already fetches canonical data per task.

3. **Manual reconcile button**: In the KanbanBoard header, add a 'Sync' button that calls `POST /api/kanban/sync` to manually trigger reconciliation from canonical files. Currently reconcile runs automatically every 5s but a manual trigger is useful after making direct file edits.

intake.brief: Dashboard UI improvements: force-done, stuck indicators, manual reconcile
intake.refinement_summary: Identified after dashboard-0043 showed no UI way to recover stuck tasks without CLI.

plan.approach: Three focused UI + API changes to the rook-dashboard Next.js app.
scope: Force-Done action in TaskModal / KanbanCard context menu
Stuck indicator badge in KanbanCard (heartbeat age check)
Manual Sync button in KanbanBoard header

steps.title: Add Force Done button to TaskModal actions
Add stuck-task amber indicator to KanbanCard when heartbeat age > 30m
Add manual Sync button to KanbanBoard header

checklist.title: Force Done UI action in TaskModal
